newsSince: No clearly new company-specific news since June 9. Acuvi's identifiable 2026 catalysts (a strategic development order from a Formula 1 customer, a ~$1.9M US-defense order over 30 months, and the Q1 2026 cost-pressure earnings call) all date to earlier in the year and appear already reflected. The ~3% drift since June 9 is consistent with low-liquidity micro-cap noise, not a fundamental change.

verdicts.rankRationale: Humanoid-stack lens: precision-motion actuation is a credible hidden-gem if content expands into hands/grippers, but no new robotics design wins since June 9 to validate the thesis. Q1 showed cost pressure; thesis intact, no fresh catalyst. Hold at #4.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Precision-motion content win into humanoid hands/grippers/sensors
verdicts.primaryRisk: Q1 cost pressure, $40M micro-cap liquidity, no robotics design-win proof yet
verdicts.bull: Genuine actuation-purity micro-cap; precision piezo motion could plug directly into humanoid hands and compact robot modules.
verdicts.neutral: Thesis remains relevant but unproven in robotics; current orders are F1/defense/medtech, not humanoids.
verdicts.bear: Tiny illiquid cap, -30% YTD, Q1 cost pressure; humanoid actuation content remains aspirational.
